channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Antitrust cases against Big Tech and megamergers could end in deals under Trump, experts say
item.description: The U.S. antitrust watchdogs that pounced on Big Tech and deterred corporate deal-making throughout President Joe Biden’s administration may be kept on a shorter leash by Donald Trump after he returns to the White House next year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: Everything you need to know about Trump FCC pick Brendan Carr
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump chose Brendan Carr to lead the Federal Communications Commission, the agency that regulates broadcasting, telecommunications, and broadband. Here’s what you need to know about the cabinet pick. He’s a longtime FCC member Carr has history with the FCC.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: How ATO as Code Can Reduce Compliance Burden
item.description: Learn how ATO as code can help reduce the compliance burden for government agencies and organizations.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Nvidia Blackwell chips face serious heating issues
item.description: Nvidia’s next-generation Blackwell data center processors have significant problems with overheating when installed in high-capacity server racks, forcing redesigns of the racks themselves, according to a report by The Information.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How Trump’s appointment of Brendan Carr as FCC chairman will affect enterprises
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump has announced Brendan Carr as the next head of the US Federal Communications Commission (FCC), the organization responsible for setting the regulations governing telecoms, broadband, satellite communications and broadcasting in the US.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: AI platforms driving business transformation in the UAE: insights from industry experts
item.description: In today’s fast-paced digital landscape, AI platforms are playing a pivotal role in reshaping industries and driving business transformation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Windows 10 Insider Previews: A guide to the builds
item.description: Microsoft never sleeps. In addition to its steady releases of major and minor updates to the current version of Windows 10, the company frequently rolls out public preview builds to members of its Windows Insider Program, allowing them to test out — and even help shape — upcoming features.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Banking on AI: How Financial Institutions are Leveraging Technology for Customer Experience Transformation
item.description: Banks are increasingly relying on AI, machine learning, and generative AI to improve customer experience and stay competitive in a rapidly changing market.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 14 great preprocessors for developers who love to code
item.description: As much as we love them, programming languages can often feel like straitjackets. They’re a complex bundle of syntactic rules, and if we break them–even once–the compiler starts screaming out error messages.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Middle East's Tech Sector Gets Boost with CIO100 Awards Launch
item.description: Foundry and IDC launch inaugural CIO100 Middle East Awards, recognizing visionary tech leaders
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Mobile AI opens new horizons for sustainable business growth in the digital age
item.description: As we navigate through 2024 and gear up for 2025, we are witnessing a pivotal moment in technological evolution where two transformative forces—5.5G (also referred to as 5G-Advanced or 5G-A) networks and artificial intelligence (AI)—are converging to reshape our digital landscape.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Lawsuit alleging Elon Musk rigged Dogecoin is dropped
item.description: A lawsuit accusing Elon Musk of rigging Dogecoin is ending. Investors in the cryptocurrency who said the world’s richest person and his electric car company Tesla committed fraud and insider trading are withdrawing their appeal from an Aug. 29 dismissal of their case.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: Democratic senators call for federal probe into Musk's contact with Russia
item.description: A pair of Democratic senators called for a federal investigation into Elon Musk’s involvement in SpaceX’s federal contracts following reports the tech billionaire has been in regular contact with Russian President Vladimir Putin.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: For November, Patch Tuesday includes three Windows zero-day fixes
item.description: Microsoft’s November Patch Tuesday release addresses 89 vulnerabilities in Windows, SQL Server, .NET and Microsoft Office — and three zero-day vulnerabilities (CVE-2024-43451, CVE-2024-49019 and CVE-2024-49039) that mean a patch now recommendation for Windows platforms.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Psaki: 'Disinformation space' on social media a 'core' issue behind Harris defeat
item.description: Former White House press secretary Jen Psaki is blaming Vice President Harris's loss to President-elect Trump in part on disinformation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Apple announces new feature to help airlines track your lost luggage
item.description: For air travelers who have popped an AirTag in their luggage – but still struggle to get help when suitcases are misplaced – this software update is for you.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, NewsNation

item.title: What the Trump election means for Microsoft’s AI dreams
item.description: With last week’s US elections, everything changed for the tech industry, especially for Microsoft. Given President-elect Donald J.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Bitcoin neared $90,000 in a new record high. What to know about crypto's post-election rally
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As money continues to pour into crypto following Donald Trump's reelection last week, bitcoin has climbed to yet another record high. The world's largest cryptocurrency topped $89,000 for the first time, briefly peaking at $89,995 early Tuesday, according to CoinDesk.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Facebook and Instagram users in Europe can opt for less personalized ads
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Facebook and Instagram users in Europe will get the option to see less personalized ads if they don't want to pay for an ad-free subscription, social media company Meta said Tuesday, bowing to pressure from Brussels over privacy and digital competition concerns.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Alarming rise of fake legal requests: What it means for your privacy
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says there’s been a rise in cybercriminal services using hacked police and government emails to send subpoenas and data requests to U.S. companies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
